summary: The text discusses cryptographic methods, including cipher tables and planetary associations, with instructions for encoding using doubled letters and numerical systems.

entities.name: alphabet
entities.description: Used in cipher systems with specific letter assignments and doubling rules
entities.quote: the alphabet may be used by any sign or planet

entities.name: doubled letters
entities.description: Each letter should be doubled to prevent misreading and false strikes
entities.quote: each letter may be doubled, lest it be read falsely

entities.name: 19 letters
entities.description: Recommended alphabet size to avoid duplication and confusion
entities.quote: I wish the alphabet to consist of 19 letters, not duplicating 18

entities.name: planetary characters
entities.description: Linked to cipher systems and numbered signs for cryptographic use
entities.quote: tablets marked with numbers {cryptic @ signs or planetary characters}

entities.name: chemical analysis table
entities.description: A fixed table for varied reading, referenced on folio 60

entities.name: twelve signs
entities.description: Assigned numbers in cipher systems, possibly zodiac-related
entities.quote: We assign numbers to the twelve signs.

entities.name: Polydora
entities.description: Cited author of prior cryptographic work

entities.name: furnace
entities.description: Metaphorical location for cryptographic 'work' or transformation
entities.quote: we have driven into the furnace in which

entities.name: wandering iron
entities.description: Symbolic element concluding the cryptographic process

entities.name: tablets marked with numbers
entities.description: Physical carriers for cryptographic symbols and planetary characters
entities.quote: tablets marked with numbers {cryptic @ signs or planetary characters}
